title: Kubernetes Purpose, Architecture, Reconciliation, and Environment Evidence · API Object Lens: K8s Api Etcd Source Truth
summary: A civic platform team must decide which availability problem Kubernetes can solve without mistaking orchestration for application correctness or infrastructure durability. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s api etcd source truth increment in cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain how API requests, admission, persisted desired state, watches, caches, and component status relate without treating any one cache as authority.
Trace API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, container runtime, network proxy, DNS, and add-on responsibilities for one workload transition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Api Etcd Source Truth · Theory K8s Api Etcd Source Truth
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness. A defensible solution applies K8s Api Etcd Source Truth, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-architecture-reconciliation-evidence-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-architecture-reconciliation-evidence-3
    artifact: architecture-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked k8s architecture reconciliation evidence 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      request-path: kubectl -> api-server -> admission -> etcd
      controller-watch: desired-vs-observed
      node-path: scheduler -> kubelet -> runtime
      convergence: status-condition
    verify: kubectl version && kubectl cluster-info && kubectl get --raw /readyz?verbose
    failure: Reject client-version-only evidence, direct-etcd application access, or a claim that reconciliation guarantees application correctness.
    repair: Capture current context, server APIs, component authority, desired state, observed status, and the remaining distribution-specific boundary.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
